# Storage regression snapshot — v1.1.1

Client storage generated at `v1.1.1`, the release that fixes the EUDI database
being opened without its AES key. Loaded and verified by
`TestClientStorageRegressionV1_1_1`. Regenerate with
`TestGenerateClientStorageForRegressionTests` (`GENERATE_STORAGE=1`) while
`version.go` reads `1.1.1`.

Unlike the `v1.0.0` snapshot (whose `eudi_client_db` is intentionally plaintext to
exercise the migration), this snapshot's `eudi_client_db` is **born encrypted** — it
was written by the fixed code. Loading it therefore exercises the steady-state
encrypted read path: no plaintext migration runs, and the credentials/logs must read
back through the SQLCipher connection. The test asserts the file is not plaintext
(`sqlcipher.IsPlaintext` is false).

## Files

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `bbolt_client_db` | IRMA client bbolt database (idemix credentials, IRMA-issued SD-JWTs, logs). Copied to `db2` on load. |
| `eudi_client_db` | EUDI SQLCipher database (`yivi-eudi.db`): OpenID4VCI credentials. **Encrypted at rest.** |
| `ecdsa_sk.pem` | Client signer key. |
| `keyshare_users.json` | Keyshare users preloaded into the test keyshare server. |
| `metadata.json` | Human-readable dump of the stored credentials and logs. |

## Contents

Generated by the same session script as the `v1.0.0` snapshot (see
`TestGenerateClientStorageForRegressionTests`), so the credential and log contents
match; the only intended difference is that the EUDI database is encrypted. The
`v1.1.1` regression test checks a representative subset (both OpenID4VCI credentials,
the SD-JWT instance count, and all four log types, newest-first); the exhaustive
per-attribute assertions live in `TestClientStorageRegressionV1_0_0`.
